[precondition:es_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'es_env_ready':apply] $ /bin/sh -c '
set -e
TMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
kubectl -n elasticsearch exec openssl-toolbox -- /bin/sh -c '\''
set -e
mkdir -p /tmp/certs
cat > /tmp/certs/openssl.cnf <<EOF
distinguished_name=req_distinguished_name
req_extensions=v3_req
prompt=no
[req_distinguished_name]
CN=es-http
[v3_req]
keyUsage=critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage=serverAuth,clientAuth
subjectAltName=@alt_names
[alt_names]
DNS.1=localhost
DNS.2=es-http
DNS.3=es-http.elasticsearch
DNS.4=es-http.elasticsearch.svc
DNS.5=es-http.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
DNS.6=es-cluster
DNS.7=es-cluster.elasticsearch
DNS.8=es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc
DNS.9=es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
DNS.10=es-cluster-0
DNS.11=es-cluster-1
DNS.12=es-cluster-2
DNS.13=es-cluster-0.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
DNS.14=es-cluster-1.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
DNS.15=es-cluster-2.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
EOF
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/ca.key 2048
openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key /tmp/certs/ca.key -sha256 -days 365 -subj "/CN=es-ca" -out /tmp/certs/ca.crt
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/server.key 2048
openssl req -new -key /tmp/certs/server.key -out /tmp/certs/server.csr -config /tmp/certs/openssl.cnf
openssl x509 -req -in /tmp/certs/server.csr -CA /tmp/certs/ca.crt -CAkey /tmp/certs/ca.key -CAcreateserial -out /tmp/certs/server.crt -days 365 -extensions v3_req -extfile /tmp/certs/openssl.cnf
'\''
kubectl -n elasticsearch cp openssl-toolbox:/tmp/certs "${TMP_DIR}/certs"
kubectl -n elasticsearch create secret generic es-http-tls --from-file=tls.crt="${TMP_DIR}/certs/server.crt" --from-file=tls.key="${TMP_DIR}/certs/server.key" --from-file=ca.crt="${TMP_DIR}/certs/ca.crt" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
'

: > /tmp/ops/users

ES_PATH_CONF=/tmp/ops /usr/share/elasticsearch/bin/elasticsearch-users useradd ops-user -p opspass -r superuser
ES_PATH_CONF=/tmp/report /usr/share/elasticsearch/bin/elasticsearch-users useradd report-user -p reportpass -r viewer
: > /tmp/ops/roles.yml

'\''

kubectl -n elasticsearch cp file-realm-gen:/tmp/report "${TMP_DIR}/report"
kubectl -n elasticsearch create secret generic es-file-realm-aggregate --from-file=users="${TMP_DIR}/ops/users" --from-file=users_roles="${TMP_DIR}/ops/users_roles" --from-file=roles.yml="${TMP_DIR}/ops/roles.yml" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

content.content:
1	apiVersion: apps/v1
2	kind: StatefulSet
3	metadata:
4	  name: es-cluster
5	  namespace: elasticsearch
6	spec:
7	  serviceName: es-cluster
8	  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
9	  replicas: 3
10	  selector:
11	    matchLabels:
12	      app: es-cluster
13	  template:
14	    metadata:
15	      labels:
16	        app: es-cluster
17	    spec:
18	      containers:
19	        - name: elasticsearch
20	          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:7.17.9
21	          env:
22	            - name: POD_NAME
23	              valueFrom:
24	                fieldRef:
25	                  fieldPath: metadata.name
26	            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
27	              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
28	            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
29	              value: "elasticpass"
30	          ports:
31	            - containerPort: 9200
32	              name: http
33	            - containerPort: 9300
34	              name: transport
35	          readinessProbe:
36	            tcpSocket:
37	              port: 9200
38	            initialDelaySeconds: 10
39	            periodSeconds: 5
40	            timeoutSeconds: 1
41	            failureThreshold: 6
42	          volumeMounts:
43	            - name: es-config
44	              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
45	              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
46	            - name: data
47	              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
48	            - name: http-certs
49	              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs
50	              readOnly: true
51	            - name: file-realm
52	              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/users
53	              subPath: users
54	              readOnly: true
55	            - name: file-realm
56	              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/users_roles
57	              subPath: users_roles
58	              readOnly: true
59	            - name: file-realm
60	              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/roles.yml
61	              subPath: roles.yml
62	              readOnly: true
63	          resources:
64	            requests:
65	              cpu: "500m"
66	              memory: "1Gi"
67	            limits:
68	              cpu: "1"
69	              memory: "2Gi"
70	      volumes:
71	        - name: es-config
72	          configMap:
73	            name: es-config
74	            items:

file.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es-cluster
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-cluster
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: es-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: es-cluster
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:7.17.9
          env:
            - name: POD_NAME
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.name
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              value: "elasticpass"
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9200
              name: http
            - containerPort: 9300
              name: transport
          readinessProbe:
            tcpSocket:
              port: 9200
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 5
            timeoutSeconds: 1
            failureThreshold: 6
          volumeMounts:
            - name: es-config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
            - name: http-certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs
              readOnly: true
            - name: file-realm
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/users
              subPath: users
              readOnly: true
            - name: file-realm
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/users_roles
              subPath: users_roles
              readOnly: true
            - name: file-realm
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/roles.yml
              subPath: roles.yml
              readOnly: true
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "500m"
              memory: "1Gi"
            limits:
              cpu: "1"
              memory: "2Gi"
      volumes:
        - name: es-config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
            items:
              - key: elasticsearch.yml
                path: elasticsearch.yml
        - name: http-certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
        - name: file-realm
          secret:
            secretName: es-file-realm-aggregate
            items:
              - key: users
                path: users
              - key: users_roles
                path: users_roles
              - key: roles.yml
                path: roles.yml
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 5Gi

file.content:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${POD_NAME}
    node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - es-cluster-0.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - es-cluster-1.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - es-cluster-2.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - es-cluster-0
      - es-cluster-1
      - es-cluster-2
    node.store.allow_mmap: false
    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.authc.realms.file.file1.order: 0
    xpack.security.authc.realms.native.native1.order: 1
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: ["/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/ca.crt"]
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: ["/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/ca.crt"]

content.thinking:
Now I understand the setup. Let me check what's in the secrets:

1. `es-file-realm-aggregate` - the secret mounted into the ES StatefulSet for the file realm (contains `ops-user`)
2. `user-provided-file-realm` - a separate secret with `report-user`

The problem is clear: the StatefulSet only mounts `es-file-realm-aggregate`, which contains `ops-user` but NOT `report-user`. The `user-provided-file-realm` secret contains `report-user` but it's not merged into the aggregate secret.
So the task is to merge `user-provided-file-realm` into `es-file-realm-aggregate` and make sure the StatefulSet uses the combined secret. Let me look at what's actually in both secrets to see how to combine them properly.

content.text: Now I understand the setup. The StatefulSet mounts `es-file-realm-aggregate` (which has `ops-user`) but `report-user` is only in `user-provided-file-realm`. I need to merge both secrets.

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture. The `es-file-realm-aggregate` secret only has `ops-user`, and `user-provided-file-realm` has `report-user`. The StatefulSet mounts `es-file-realm-aggregate`.

I need to merge both secrets into the `es-file-realm-aggregate` secret by combining:
- `users`: both lines concatenated
- `users_roles`: both lines concatenated
- `roles.yml`: empty (both are empty)

Then update the secret. The StatefulSet will automatically pick up the new secret data (Kubernetes secrets mounted as volumes are updated automatically, but the file realm in Elasticsearch needs to either be reloaded or the pods restarted).
Kubernetes will automatically sync the updated secret files to the mounted volumes within a few seconds, and I can use Elasticsearch's security reload API to apply the changes without restarting the pods.

content.text: The `es-file-realm-aggregate` secret only has `ops-user` — `report-user` from `user-provided-file-realm` was never merged in. I'll combine both and patch the aggregate secret, then trigger a security reload.

input.command:
# Merge users files
USERS_MERGED=$(printf '%s\n%s' \
  "$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret es-file-realm-aggregate -o jsonpath='{.data.users}' | base64 -d)" \
  "$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret user-provided-file-realm -o jsonpath='{.data.users}' | base64 -d)")

# Merge users_roles files
ROLES_MERGED=$(printf '%s\n%s' \
  "$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret es-file-realm-aggregate -o jsonpath='{.data.users_roles}' | base64 -d)" \
  "$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret user-provided-file-realm -o jsonpath='{.data.users_roles}' | base64 -d)")

# roles.yml is empty in both, keep empty
ROLES_YML=""

echo "=== Merged users ==="
echo "$USERS_MERGED"
echo "=== Merged users_roles ==="
echo "$ROLES_MERGED"

# Patch the secret with merged content
kubectl -n elasticsearch create secret generic es-file-realm-aggregate \
  --from-literal=users="$USERS_MERGED" \
  --from-literal=users_roles="$ROLES_MERGED" \
  --from-literal=roles.yml="$ROLES_YML" \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
input.description: Merge report-user into the aggregate file realm secret

content.text: Secret updated. Now wait for Kubernetes to propagate the updated secret to the pods (mounted secrets auto-update), then trigger ES to reload its security settings.

input.command:
# Wait for the mounted files to update in the pods (up to ~60s)
echo "Waiting for secret propagation..."
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  CONTENT=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch exec es-cluster-0 -- cat /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/users 2>/dev/null)
  if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -q "report-user"; then
    echo "Secret propagated after ${i}x2s"
    break
  fi
  sleep 2
done
echo "Current users file in es-cluster-0:"
kubectl -n elasticsearch exec es-cluster-0 -- cat /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/users

content.thinking:
The secret hasn't propagated yet. The file realm secret is mounted as a `subPath` volume, which is the reason it's not auto-updating! When a secret is mounted with `subPath`, Kubernetes does NOT automatically update the file when the secret changes. This is a well-known Kubernetes limitation.

From the StatefulSet spec:
```yaml
- name: file-realm
  mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/users
  subPath: users
  readOnly: true
- name: file-realm
  mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/users_roles
  subPath: users_roles
  readOnly: true
- name: file-realm
  mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/roles.yml
  subPath: roles.yml
  readOnly: true
```

All three config files are mounted with `subPath`, so none of the changes will propagate automatically. I need to either restart the pods to pick up the updated secret, or remove the `subPath` mounts entirely. Since the question emphasizes keeping the cluster healthy and not breaking existing users, a rolling restart seems like the safer approach.

I'll trigger a rolling restart of the StatefulSet using kubectl.

content.text: The secret mounts use `subPath`, which means Kubernetes won't auto-propagate updates. I need to do a rolling restart of the StatefulSet.

input.command:
kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -w --timeout=300s 2>&1 | head -40 &
# Poll every 10s until rollout done
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch rollout status statefulset/es-cluster 2>&1)
  echo "[${i}] $STATUS"
  if echo "$STATUS" | grep -q "successfully rolled out"; then
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
